complaint.description: I paid for a consultation package but experienced a three-week delay in receiving my MRI results, causing anxiety and delaying treatment.
complaint.customerImpactSummary: Patient reports significant anxiety and a delay to onward treatment planning following an extended wait for MRI imaging results that exceeded the published 5-working-day turnaround.

consultations.notes: Patient presenting with persistent right knee pain. Recommend MRI scan to rule out meniscal tear.
Follow-up after delayed MRI. Results reviewed; partial medial meniscus tear confirmed. Discussed conservative management.

treatmentTimeline.detail: Promised call-back the next day; not received

policies.title: Diagnostic Imaging Service Standard
policies.excerpt: Routine MRI results will be made available to the referring consultant within 5 working days of the scan. Patients should be proactively contacted if a delay is anticipated.

policies.title: Patient Communication Policy
policies.excerpt: Where service standards cannot be met, the patient must be informed within 24 hours with a revised expected date and a named point of contact.

policies.title: Complaint Handling Policy
policies.excerpt: All complaints will be acknowledged within 2 working days and resolved within 20 working days. Goodwill gestures may be offered where service failure is established.

policies.title: Premium Private Healthcare Package Terms
policies.excerpt: Includes priority diagnostic scheduling and a dedicated patient liaison for the duration of any open episode of care.

emails.subject: MRI results — chasing
emails.excerpt: It has now been 13 days since my MRI. I was told to expect results within 5 working days. Please can someone confirm when I will hear back?

emails.subject: Re: MRI results — chasing (2)
emails.excerpt: I called yesterday and was promised a call-back. I have not heard anything. The wait is causing significant anxiety.

emails.subject: Re: MRI results
emails.excerpt: Apologies for the delay. Your results have now been released to Dr. Hartley. An appointment has been booked for 12 May.

chats.excerpt: I can see the MRI was performed on 14 April. I am unable to see results on file. I will escalate to Imaging and someone will call you tomorrow.

calls.summary: Patient enquiring about delayed MRI results. Promised call-back from radiology lead within 24h. Call-back did not occur.

internalNotes.author: Hannah Roberts (Complaints Reviewer)
internalNotes.note: Imaging team confirmed results were ready on 22 April but not released due to administrative backlog. No proactive comms to patient.

internalNotes.author: Imaging Lead
internalNotes.note: Acknowledged 5-day SLA was not met. Reviewing release-workflow gap.

justification.serviceAssessment: Patient experienced a 21-day wait for MRI results against a published 5-working-day standard — a 14-day breach. Internal notes confirm results were available on 22 April but not released until 5 May due to an administrative backlog.
justification.customerImpact: Patient contacted support on at least three occasions (one email chase, one call, one follow-up email) and reported anxiety and a delay to onward treatment planning. A promised call-back was not delivered.
justification.policyReview: The delay is inconsistent with NH-SVC-014 (Imaging) and NH-COMM-002 (Patient Communication), which requires proactive notification within 24h of a foreseeable delay. Premium package terms (NH-PKG-PRV) further entitle the patient to priority handling, which was not evident.

recommendedActions: Issue a formal written apology to the patient citing the specific SLA breach.
Offer a goodwill gesture (e.g., consultation-fee credit or refund of the imaging element).
Initiate a service review of the imaging-results release workflow.
Open a process-improvement investigation to prevent recurrence and report back within 20 working days.

insights.title: Service Delay
insights.description: MRI results released 14 working days after the 5-day SLA.

insights.title: Communication Failure
insights.description: Three patient-initiated contacts; one undelivered promised call-back.

insights.title: Policy Non-Compliance
insights.description: Premium package priority-handling commitment not honoured.

complaint.description: I cancelled my membership but continued to be charged for three additional months.
complaint.customerImpactSummary: Member reports an in-club cancellation request was made but three further monthly direct debits were taken. Member states the cancellation form was completed but no acknowledgment was received.

membershipActivity.activity: Last gym visit before cancellation request
Member emailed billing team querying charges

membershipChanges.change: Membership marked as cancelled in CRM

policies.title: Gym Membership Cancellation Policy
policies.excerpt: Members may cancel by completing the in-club cancellation form or by writing to membership services. A 30-day notice period applies; one further monthly payment may be collected within the notice period.

policies.title: Cancellation Acknowledgment Standard
policies.excerpt: All cancellation requests must be acknowledged in writing within 5 working days, confirming the effective end date and any final payment.

policies.title: Refund Policy — Membership Fees
policies.excerpt: Where charges are collected after a valid cancellation request, the member is entitled to a refund of the over-collected amount within 10 working days of confirmation.

policies.title: Complaint Handling Policy
policies.excerpt: All complaints will be acknowledged within 2 working days and resolved within 20 working days.

emails.subject: I cancelled in February — still being charged
emails.excerpt: I completed the cancellation form in club on 1 February. I am still being charged. I have screenshots of the bank statements. Please refund.

emails.subject: Re: Cancellation enquiry
emails.excerpt: We are unable to locate a cancellation request before 18 April. Per the 30-day notice period one further payment may apply.

calls.summary: Member explained the February in-club form. Agent unable to find the form. Promised to escalate to club manager.

internalNotes.author: Bristol Club Manager
internalNotes.note: Located the February cancellation form filed under the wrong month. Acknowledgment was never issued.

internalNotes.author: Marcus Wells (Complaints Reviewer)
internalNotes.note: Per policy, one payment is owed for the 30-day notice period; three subsequent payments are over-collection.

justification.serviceAssessment: An in-club cancellation form was completed on 1 February and subsequently misfiled. The acknowledgment required by NH-GYM-008 was not issued. Four monthly payments were taken after the cancellation request.
justification.customerImpact: Member made multiple contacts and supplied bank-statement evidence. Reports frustration and time spent chasing.
justification.policyReview: Per NH-GYM-007 a 30-day notice period applies, so one of the four collected payments is contractually due. Three payments are over-collection and refundable under NH-FIN-003.

recommendedActions: Confirm cancellation effective 1 March 2026 (30 days after 1 February).
Refund three monthly payments (£192.00 in total) within 10 working days.
Issue a written apology covering the misfiling and the lack of acknowledgment.
Review the in-club cancellation-form intake process at the Bristol club.

insights.title: Process Failure
insights.description: In-club cancellation form misfiled; no acknowledgment issued.

insights.title: Billing Over-Collection
insights.description: Three monthly payments collected after the contractual end date.

complaint.description: My physiotherapy treatment was declined despite receiving a consultant referral.
complaint.customerImpactSummary: Member states physiotherapy was declined after a valid consultant referral. Member is paying out-of-pocket and is concerned about the impact on rehabilitation.

claims.notes: Decline reason on letter: 'No prior authorisation on file.'

policies.title: Comprehensive Plan — Outpatient Physiotherapy
policies.excerpt: Outpatient physiotherapy is covered when supported by a consultant referral. Prior authorisation is required for courses exceeding 4 sessions.

policies.title: Prior Authorisation Workflow
policies.excerpt: When a consultant referral indicates a treatment course requiring prior authorisation, the insurer will request the missing authorisation rather than declining the claim outright, where the referral is otherwise valid.

policies.title: Claims Decision Communication
policies.excerpt: Decline letters must specify the policy section and indicate any remediation route (e.g., submission of missing authorisation) before final decline.

policies.title: Complaint Handling Policy
policies.excerpt: All complaints will be acknowledged within 2 working days and resolved within 20 working days.

emails.subject: Physiotherapy claim declined
emails.excerpt: I have a consultant referral from Mr. Singh and was told physiotherapy is covered. The decline letter just says 'no prior authorisation'. No one asked me for one.

emails.subject: Re: Physiotherapy claim declined
emails.excerpt: Our records show no prior authorisation on file. You may resubmit with authorisation; however the original claim remains declined.

chats.excerpt: Confirmed referral is on file and valid. Course is 6 sessions which exceeds the 4-session threshold and therefore needs prior auth. We can re-process if authorisation is obtained.

internalNotes.author: Olivia Carter (Complaints Reviewer)
internalNotes.note: Referral is valid. The decline letter did not signpost the missing-authorisation remediation route required by NH-INS-022 and NH-INS-023.

justification.serviceAssessment: A valid consultant referral was on file. The claim was declined for missing prior authorisation, which is required because the course exceeds 4 sessions. However, the decline communication did not follow NH-INS-022, which directs the insurer to request the missing authorisation before final decline.
justification.customerImpact: Member is paying out-of-pocket and has expressed concern about delayed rehabilitation. Multiple contacts have been needed to clarify the route forward.
justification.policyReview: The substantive decline is policy-correct (prior authorisation absent), but the process failed NH-INS-022 and NH-INS-023. The complaint is partially upheld on process grounds.

recommendedActions: Re-open the claim and request prior authorisation from the consultant on the member's behalf.
Reimburse any sessions undertaken after the original referral, subject to standard cover limits, once authorisation is in place.
Issue a written apology for the process failure in the decline letter.
Audit decline letters issued in the prior 90 days for similar signposting gaps.

insights.title: Process Failure
insights.description: Decline issued without signposting the authorisation-remediation route.

insights.title: Member Communication
insights.description: Two emails and a chat needed to surface the correct next step.

insights.title: Audit Exposure
insights.description: Similar decline-letter patterns may exist; broader audit recommended.
